searchDocs.title: QA ZERO Architecture
searchDocs.keywords: QA ZERO  WordPress  analytics  heatmap  documentation

searchDocs.title: System OverviewтАЛ
searchDocs.content: QA ZERO consists of several key components:  Core Tracking System: JavaScript code that collects user interaction dataData Storage: File-based and database storage for collected dataData Processing: Background processes that analyze and aggregate dataAdmin Interface: WordPress admin panels for viewing analytics and configuring the system

searchDocs.title: Core Tracking SystemтАЛ
searchDocs.content: The tracking system is implemented as JavaScript that is injected into WordPress pages. It:  Captures page views, clicks, scrolls, and other user interactionsSends data to the server via AJAX callsHandles user consent and privacy settings

searchDocs.title: Data StorageтАЛ
searchDocs.content: QA ZERO uses a hybrid storage approach:  Raw data is stored in files in the wp-content/qa-zero-data directoryProcessed data is stored in WordPress database tablesSession recordings are stored as compressed JSON files

searchDocs.title: Data ProcessingтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Data processing is primarily handled by WordPress cron jobs that:  Aggregate raw data into meaningful metricsGenerate heatmaps from click and scroll dataCalculate conversion rates and other KPIs

searchDocs.title: Admin InterfaceтАЛ
searchDocs.content: The admin interface is integrated into the WordPress admin panel and provides:  Dashboard widgets for quick insightsDetailed reports and visualizationsConfiguration options for tracking and privacy settings

searchDocs.title: Data FlowтАЛ
searchDocs.content: User visits a page with QA ZERO tracking codeJavaScript collects interaction dataData is sent to the server via AJAXRaw data is stored in filesCron jobs process the raw dataProcessed data is stored in the databaseAdmin interface displays the processed data

searchDocs.title: Coming SoonтАЛ
searchDocs.content: More detailed architecture documentation will be added in future updates.

searchDocs.title: API Documentation Version Management
searchDocs.keywords: QA ZERO  WordPress  analytics  heatmap  documentation

searchDocs.title: Two-Layer Versioning (Version + Update)тАЛ
searchDocs.content: QA ZERO API uses a two-layer versioning scheme. Understanding this is the most important thing in this document тАФ get it wrong and you will either break clients or hide new features from them.  Layer	Format	Changes whenтАж	VisibilityVersion	YYYY-MM-DD	Only on breaking changes. Response shape, required fields, semantics change.	Appears in the URL (?version=2025-10-20) and in the directory name (/developer-manual/api/2025-10-20/). Supported for 24 months after release. Update (api_update)	YYYY-MM-DD	Any backward-compatible addition тАФ new material, new filter operator, new calc function, new features flag, new optional field.	Lives inside a version. Returned by /guide as the api_update field. Does not change the URL or directory name.  Canonical name: "Version 2025-10-20, update 2026-04-11" тАФ this is how we refer to a specific API revision in tickets, commits, and release notes.

searchDocs.title: Why two layers?тАЛ
searchDocs.content: Clients hate URL churn. If version changed every time a feature shipped, every client would have to re-pin. We want version to feel stable.We still need to tell clients what's available. When a new operator like between ships, AI agents and SDKs need to know it exists тАФ that's what api_update + the features map in /guide are for.It mirrors how Stripe, AWS, and other mature APIs do it. Living documentation + Since: YYYY-MM-DD inline badges + a machine-readable feature map.

searchDocs.title: One version = one living documentтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Do not duplicate the 2025-10-20/ directory every time you bump api_update. The directory is a single living document for the entire lifetime of version 2025-10-20. When you add a feature:  Add a **Since:** YYYY-MM-DD badge to the feature's section in qal.md / materials.md / endpoints.mdAdd a new entry at the top of the Update Ledger (Changelog) in index.mdBump api_update and last_updated in the frontmatter of every edited fileBump QAHM_API_UPDATE in the qa-labo plugin source so /guide reports the new date  If you ever catch yourself typing cp -r 2025-10-20 2025-10-20-update-xxxx, stop. That's wrong.

searchDocs.title: The features map is the runtime truthтАЛ
searchDocs.content: The /guide response includes a features object keyed by QAL/result feature name (filter, join, calc, view_chaining, sort, sample, include_count, return_file, return_csv, return_parquet). Each value is a boolean indicating whether the executor actually processes that feature on this server.  Clients must read features from /guide at startup rather than hard-coding feature availability.Documentation claims that contradict features are always wrong тАФ the plugin's qal-validation-{version}.yaml is the authoritative source and the plugin publishes it through /guide.When you add a feature, flip its flag in that YAML and bump QAHM_API_UPDATE. The doc should follow.

searchDocs.title: Version FormatтАЛ
searchDocs.content: API Version: YYYY-MM-DD (Calendar versioning тАФ rare, breaking only)API Update (api_update): YYYY-MM-DD (Calendar versioning тАФ frequent, non-breaking)Documentation Version: MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH (Semantic versioning, internal to this docs repo)

searchDocs.title: 1. When Making Documentation ChangesтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Update both last_updated and api_update in the frontmatter of modified files. api_update is machine-readable and should match the QAHM_API_UPDATE constant in the qa-labo plugin source.  --- id: getting-started-2025-10-20 title: Getting Started sidebar_position: 1 last_updated: 2026-04-11 # тЖР Human-readable "last touched" date api_update: 2026-04-11 # тЖР Matches plugin's QAHM_API_UPDATE constant ---   For content describing a specific feature, add an inline Since badge so readers can tell when it shipped:  ### `filter` тАФ row-level filter **Since:** 2026-04-10 The `filter` key accepts a flat map of column тЖТ condition...

searchDocs.title: 2. Update ChangelogтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Add entries to the Changelog section in index.md:  ### YYYY-MM-DD - Documentation vX.Y.Z **Added:** - тЬЕ New feature description **Removed:** - тЭМ Deprecated feature **Changed:** - ЁЯФД Modified feature

searchDocs.title: 3. Documentation Version BumpingтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Follow semantic versioning for documentation:  MAJOR (1.0.0 тЖТ 2.0.0): Breaking changes in documentation structureMINOR (1.0.0 тЖТ 1.1.0): New content added (new sections, endpoints, features)PATCH (1.0.0 тЖТ 1.0.1): Fixes, clarifications, typo corrections

searchDocs.title: Current VersionsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: API Version	Current api_update	Doc Version	Status	Released	Support Until2025-10-20	2026-04-11	1.1.2	Current	2025-10-20	2027-10-20  The api_update column is the latest non-breaking revision documented here. The actual runtime value is whatever the target server reports in /guide тАФ a client talking to a stale plugin will see an older api_update and should adjust its expectations via the features map.

searchDocs.title: Frontmatter (Machine-readable)тАЛ
searchDocs.content: All .md files in /developer/api/2025-10-20/ contain:  last_updated: YYYY-MM-DD

searchDocs.title: Changelog (Human-readable)тАЛ
searchDocs.content: /developer/api/2025-10-20/index.md - Contains full changelog

searchDocs.title: ConfigurationтАЛ
searchDocs.content: docusaurus.config.js - Enables showLastUpdateTime: true

searchDocs.title: How It WorksтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Frontmatter last_updated: Docusaurus reads this fieldGenerates new build with updated timestampsCDN/browser cache keys change automatically Info Box: Visible version info at top of Getting Started pageShows both API and Documentation versions Changelog Section: Detailed change historyHelps users understand what changed

searchDocs.title: For CDN/Cache SystemsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: The last_updated field in frontmatter affects:  Build output filenames (Docusaurus hash)Metadata sent to clientsCDN cache invalidation triggers

searchDocs.title: Example Update FlowтАЛ
searchDocs.content: # 1. Make documentation changes vim developer/api/2025-10-20/endpoints.md # 2. Update frontmatter # Change: last_updated: 2025-10-06 # 3. Update changelog in index.md # Add entry under "### 2025-10-06 - Documentation v1.0.1" # 4. Rebuild npm run build # 5. Deploy # New hashed files automatically bust cache

searchDocs.title: Best PracticesтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Always update last_updated when modifying contentUse consistent date format: YYYY-MM-DDKeep changelog detailed but conciseGroup related changes under single date entryUse emoji markers: тЬЕ Added, тЭМ Removed, ЁЯФД Changed

searchDocs.title: Monitoring UpdatesтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Users can:  Check Version Information box at top of Getting StartedReview Changelog section for detailsSee "Last updated" timestamp at bottom of each page (Docusaurus feature)  Systems can:  Parse last_updated from frontmatterMonitor file hashes in build outputTrack version numbers in Info box

searchDocs.title: DNS Setup Instructions for docs.qazero.com
searchDocs.keywords: QA ZERO  WordPress  analytics  heatmap  documentation

searchDocs.title: PrerequisitesтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Access to your domain's DNS settings (typically through your domain registrar)GitHub Pages site already deployed

searchDocs.title: 1. Configure GitHub PagesтАЛ
searchDocs.content: The repository is already configured to use the custom domain through:  A CNAME file in the repository with docs.qazero.comGitHub Pages settings in the repository settings

searchDocs.title: 2. DNS ConfigurationтАЛ
searchDocs.content: To point your domain to GitHub Pages, you need to add the following DNS records:  Option 1: Apex Domain Configuration (Recommended)тАЛ  If you're using the apex domain (docs.qazero.com), add these A records:  Type	Host	Value	TTLA	@	185.199.108.153	3600 A	@	185.199.109.153	3600 A	@	185.199.110.153	3600 A	@	185.199.111.153	3600  Option 2: Subdomain ConfigurationтАЛ  If docs.qazero.com is a subdomain, add a CNAME record:  Type	Host	Value	TTLCNAME	docs	quarka-org.github.io	3600

searchDocs.title: 3. Verify DNS ConfigurationтАЛ
searchDocs.content: After configuring your DNS records:  Wait for DNS propagation (can take up to 48 hours, but often completes within a few hours)Verify the configuration using: dig docs.qazero.com +noall +answer Check that the site is accessible at https://docs.qazero.com

searchDocs.title: 4. Enforce HTTPSтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Once the DNS is properly configured:  Go to the GitHub repository settingsNavigate to Pages settingsCheck the "Enforce HTTPS" option

searchDocs.title: TroubleshootingтАЛ
searchDocs.content: If the domain is not working correctly:  Verify DNS records are correctly configuredCheck that the CNAME file exists in the repositoryEnsure GitHub Pages is enabled in repository settingsCheck for any error messages in the GitHub Pages section of repository settings

searchDocs.title: Additional ResourcesтАЛ
searchDocs.content: GitHub Pages DocumentationManaging a custom domain for your GitHub Pages site

searchDocs.title: Documentation Update Guide for Version Releases
searchDocs.keywords: QA ZERO  WordPress  analytics  heatmap  documentation

searchDocs.title: ЁЯЯв Scenario A (most common): Non-breaking UpdateтАЛ
searchDocs.pageTitle: Documentation Update Guide for Version Releases
searchDocs.content: Examples: a new filter operator ships, a new material is added, allpv gains a behavioral column, the features map changes, a new calc function is whitelisted.  тЖТ Stay inside the existing YYYY-MM-DD/ directory. Do not copy it. Do not create a new one. The directory is a living document.

searchDocs.title: 3-step workflowтАЛ
searchDocs.pageTitle: Documentation Update Guide for Version Releases
searchDocs.content: In qa-labo (plugin source), bump the update date Edit the implementation (executor / validator / material loader).Update src/core/yaml/qal-validation-{version}.yaml тАФ either flip a features: flag to true, add to an enum list, or add a new rule.Bump QAHM_API_UPDATE in src/core/qahm-const.php to today's date. This is the single most important step тАФ if you forget it, clients (including AI agents) will never discover the new feature via /guide.Make sure the edited files are listed in qa-labo-override-files.txt so the nightly sync from qa-platform does not clobber them. In docs.qazero.com (this repo), reflect the new feature Edit the relevant .md file(s) inside docs/developer-manual/api/{version}/ (qal.md, materials.md, endpoints.md, qal-validation.md).Add a **Since:** YYYY-MM-DD badge next to the new feature's heading or row.Bump last_updated and api_update in the frontmatter of every file you touched.Add a new entry at the top of the Update Ledger (Changelog) in {version}/index.md: ### YYYY-MM-DD тАФ `api_update: YYYY-MM-DD` тАФ Documentation vX.Y.Z **Added:** - тЬЕ Short description of the new feature If the features map or response shape in endpoints.md is visible in the /guide response example, update the example too. Commit and push Single commit to this repo, pushed to main. GitHub Actions deploys to gh-pages automatically.Commit message convention: docs(api/{version}): update YYYY-MM-DD тАФ <one-line summary>Verify on https://docs.qazero.com after a few minutes.

searchDocs.title: What you do not do in Scenario AтАЛ
searchDocs.pageTitle: Documentation Update Guide for Version Releases
searchDocs.content: тЭМ Do not cp -r 2025-10-20 2025-10-20-update-YYYYтЭМ Do not create a new api/YYYY-MM-DD/ directoryтЭМ Do not touch compatibility.md for non-breaking updates (it tracks versions, not updates)тЭМ Do not hand-edit the features map in docs to disagree with the YAML тАФ the YAML is the source of truth

searchDocs.title: ЁЯФ┤ Scenario B (rare): New API Version (breaking change)тАЛ
searchDocs.pageTitle: Documentation Update Guide for Version Releases
searchDocs.content: Examples: response envelope shape changes, a required field is removed, a column is renamed, semantics of an operator changes.  Breaking changes are rare. Before going down this path, ask: "can I add a new field and keep the old one working?" If yes, it's Scenario A.

searchDocs.title: тШСя╕П New API Version (e.g., 2025-10-20 тЖТ 2026-03-01) тАФ BREAKING ONLYтАЛ
searchDocs.pageTitle: Documentation Update Guide for Version Releases
searchDocs.content: Create new version (Scenario B only):  Copy directory: cp -r 2025-10-20/ 2026-03-01/Update 5 files in new directory (details below)Update 2 top-level files (compatibility.md, index.md)Reset api_update to the new version's release date (the two layers are scoped per-version)Keep the old version directory for 24 months as part of the support policy

searchDocs.title: ЁЯФз File-by-File Update GuideтАЛ
searchDocs.pageTitle: Documentation Update Guide for Version Releases

searchDocs.title: 1. /developer/api/2026-03-01/index.mdтАЛ

Version Info BoxтАЛ  :::info[Version Information] **API Version:** 2026-03-01 # тЖР Change date **Plugin Version Required:** 3.1.0.0+ # тЖР Update if changed **Last Updated:** 2026-03-01 # тЖР Update date **Documentation Version:** 1.0.0 # тЖР Reset to 1.0.0 :::   C. ChangelogтАЛ  ## Changelog ### 2026-03-01 - Initial Release # тЖР Add new entry **Added:** - тЬЕ New feature description **Changed:** - ЁЯФД Modified feature **Removed:** - тЭМ Deprecated feature ### [Keep previous version entries below]

TitleтАЛ  # API Endpoints Reference (2026-03-01) # тЖР Change date   C. Response examples (if changed)тАЛ  Update /guide response example if plugin_version changed:  { "version": "2026-03-01", # тЖР Update "plugin_version": "3.1.0.0", # тЖР Update if changed ... }

Add New Features Section (if applicable)тАЛ  ### Field Changes in Plugin 3.1.0.0 **Added Fields:** - тЬЕ `depth_read` - Scroll depth tracking - тЬЕ `scroll_rate` - Scroll speed **Removed Fields:** - тЭМ `old_field` - Deprecated field   OR use section-based approach:  ### Behavioral Metrics (Plugin 3.1.0.0+) | Column | Type | Description | Indexed | |--------|------|-------------|---------| | depth_read | float | Read depth (0.0-1.0) | - | | scroll_rate | float | Scroll rate | - |

searchDocs.title: тП▒я╕П Time EstimatesтАЛ

searchDocs.content: Task	Time RequiredCopy and rename files	5 minutes Update frontmatter (4 files)	5 minutes Update version numbers	10 minutes Update compatibility.md	15 minutes Write changelog	10 minutes Add new features documentation	20-60 minutes Review and test	15 minutes Total	1-2 hours

searchDocs.title: ЁЯУЭ Common ScenariosтАЛ
searchDocs.pageTitle: Documentation Update Guide for Version Releases

searchDocs.title: Scenario 1: Minor Plugin Update (3.0.0.0 тЖТ 3.0.1.0)тАЛ
searchDocs.pageTitle: Documentation Update Guide for Version Releases
searchDocs.content: No API changes, just bug fixes  тЖТ No documentation update needed. api_update stays put.

searchDocs.title: Scenario 2: Plugin Feature Addition (3.0.0.0 тЖТ 3.1.0.0)тАЛ
searchDocs.pageTitle: Documentation Update Guide for Version Releases
searchDocs.content: New fields added to existing material, new operator, new material тАФ all backward-compatible  This is Scenario A from the top of this document. Do not create a new API version directory. Bump api_update, add **Since:** badges, update the Update Ledger, and push. Update compatibility.md only if plugin version requirements changed тАФ not for every api_update bump.

searchDocs.title: Scenario 3: Breaking Change (3.0.0.0 тЖТ 4.0.0.0)тАЛ
searchDocs.pageTitle: Documentation Update Guide for Version Releases
searchDocs.content: Field removed or behavior changed  This is Scenario B. Must create a new API version directory, reset api_update to the new release date, keep the old version directory for 24 months.

searchDocs.content: Before publishing:   All dates updated (frontmatter, titles, version boxes) Plugin version requirements correct Changelog entries added compatibility.md matrix updated Code examples tested Links between pages work Build succeeds (npm run build) Preview looks good (npm start)

searchDocs.title: General FAQ
searchDocs.keywords: QA ZERO  WordPress  analytics  heatmap  documentation

searchDocs.title: What is QA ZERO?тАЛ
searchDocs.content: QA ZERO is a WordPress plugin that provides analytics, heatmaps, and session recording functionality to help you understand how users interact with your website.

searchDocs.title: How does QA ZERO compare to Google Analytics?тАЛ
searchDocs.content: While Google Analytics provides comprehensive traffic data, QA ZERO focuses on user behavior and interaction. QA ZERO offers visual tools like heatmaps and session recordings that Google Analytics doesn't provide. Many users use both tools together for a complete picture of their website performance.

searchDocs.title: Does QA ZERO slow down my website?тАЛ
searchDocs.content: QA ZERO is designed to have minimal impact on your website's performance. The tracking script is lightweight and loads asynchronously, meaning it doesn't block your page from loading.

searchDocs.title: Is QA ZERO GDPR compliant?тАЛ
searchDocs.content: Yes, QA ZERO includes features to help you comply with GDPR and other privacy regulations, including:  Cookie consent integrationData anonymization optionsTools for handling data subject requestsData retention controls

searchDocs.title: Coming SoonтАЛ
searchDocs.content: More frequently asked questions will be added in future updates.

searchDocs.title: Tutorial Intro
searchDocs.keywords: QA ZERO  WordPress  analytics  heatmap  documentation

searchDocs.title: Getting StartedтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Get started by creating a new site.  Or try Docusaurus immediately with docusaurus.new.

searchDocs.title: What you'll needтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Node.js version 18.0 or above: When installing Node.js, you are recommended to check all checkboxes related to dependencies.

searchDocs.title: Generate a new siteтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Generate a new Docusaurus site using the classic template.  The classic template will automatically be added to your project after you run the command:  npm init docusaurus@latest my-website classic   You can type this command into Command Prompt, Powershell, Terminal, or any other integrated terminal of your code editor.  The command also installs all necessary dependencies you need to run Docusaurus.

searchDocs.title: Start your siteтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Run the development server:  cd my-website npm run start   The cd command changes the directory you're working with. In order to work with your newly created Docusaurus site, you'll need to navigate the terminal there.  The npm run start command builds your website locally and serves it through a development server, ready for you to view at http://localhost:3000/.  Open docs/intro.md (this page) and edit some lines: the site reloads automatically and displays your changes.

searchDocs.title: QA ZERO v2.X Release Notes
searchDocs.keywords: QA ZERO  WordPress  analytics  heatmap  documentation

searchDocs.title: ЁЯУК OverviewтАЛ
searchDocs.content: This release includes a total of 15 improvements, such as bug fixes, feature enhancements, performance optimizations, and documentation updates. The focus was on extending QA ZERO's file functionality, improving the heatmap feature, enhancing Google API integration, and ensuring data consistency.

searchDocs.title: ЁЯЪА Key Features and ImprovementsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: ЁЯФз System Improvements and Bug Fixes  Heatmap Feature Enhancements  Added jQuery fallback functionality Improved heatmap functionality in environments where jQuery cannot be used within iframes.Implemented native JavaScript alternatives for scroll tracking, click tracking, and mouse movement detection.Added compatibility checks and fallback handling for 8 major functions. Fixed iframe display issues for mobile heatmaps Implemented a feature to exclude QA ZERO CSS files within iframes.Resolved large text display issues in mobile views.Normalized heatmap and replay displays.

searchDocs.title: Search Implementation Security Review
searchDocs.keywords: QA ZERO  WordPress  analytics  heatmap  documentation

searchDocs.title: Implementation ChoiceтАЛ
searchDocs.content: For the QA ZERO documentation site, we have implemented the docusaurus-lunr-search plugin (version 3.6.0+) as the primary search solution. This choice was made based on the following security considerations:

searchDocs.title: Security ConsiderationsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Client-side Search: The plugin performs all search operations on the client side, eliminating the need for external API calls or server-side processing. No External Dependencies: The search functionality does not rely on external services, reducing potential attack vectors and data leakage risks. No Data Collection: Unlike some search solutions that send queries to external servers, docusaurus-lunr-search keeps all search queries and indexes local to the user's browser. Open Source: The code is open source and has been reviewed by the community, increasing the likelihood that security issues are identified and addressed. Minimal JavaScript: The plugin uses a minimal amount of JavaScript, reducing the potential attack surface.

searchDocs.title: Code Audit ResultsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: We conducted a thorough code audit of the docusaurus-lunr-search plugin with the following findings:  No Suspicious Code: No suspicious or malicious code was identified in the plugin. No Unnecessary Network Requests: The plugin does not make any unnecessary network requests or transmit search data to external servers. No Sensitive Data Handling: The plugin does not handle or store sensitive user data. Proper Input Sanitization: The plugin properly sanitizes search inputs to prevent XSS attacks.

searchDocs.title: Version ManagementтАЛ
searchDocs.content: To ensure security and stability:  Fixed Version: The plugin is locked to a specific version in package.json to prevent automatic updates that might introduce security vulnerabilities. Regular Updates: A process is in place to regularly review updates to the plugin for security improvements while maintaining version control.

searchDocs.title: Multilingual SupportтАЛ
searchDocs.content: The plugin has been configured to support both English and Japanese search, with proper handling of Japanese characters and tokenization.

searchDocs.title: Option 2: Basic Search FunctionalityтАЛ
searchDocs.content: The built-in basic search functionality of Docusaurus was considered as a fallback option. While secure, it offers limited functionality compared to the chosen solution.

searchDocs.title: Option 3: @easyops-cn/docusaurus-search-localтАЛ
searchDocs.content: This plugin was evaluated but not selected due to security concerns:  Less active maintenanceMore complex codebase with potential security issuesPotential for unnecessary external communications

searchDocs.title: ConclusionтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Based on our security review, docusaurus-lunr-search provides the best balance of functionality and security for the QA ZERO documentation site. The plugin operates entirely client-side, does not transmit data to external servers, and has been locked to a specific version to prevent security regressions from automatic updates.

searchDocs.title: Future ImprovementsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Potential future improvements to search security include:  Regular security audits of the plugin as new versions are releasedConsideration of implementing Content Security Policy (CSP) headers to further restrict potential script executionMonitoring for any security advisories related to the Lunr.js library that powers the search functionality

searchDocs.title: Create a Blog Post
searchDocs.keywords: QA ZERO  WordPress  analytics  heatmap  documentation

searchDocs.title: Create your first PostтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Create a file at blog/2021-02-28-greetings.md:  blog/2021-02-28-greetings.md --- slug: greetings title: Greetings! authors: - name: Joel Marcey title: Co-creator of Docusaurus 1 url: https://github.com/JoelMarcey image_url: https://github.com/JoelMarcey.png - name: S├йbastien Lorber title: Docusaurus maintainer url: https://sebastienlorber.com image_url: https://github.com/slorber.png tags: [greetings] --- Congratulations, you have made your first post! Feel free to play around and edit this post as much as you like.   A new blog post is now available at http://localhost:3000/blog/greetings.

searchDocs.title: Create a Document
searchDocs.keywords: QA ZERO  WordPress  analytics  heatmap  documentation

searchDocs.title: Create your first DocтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Create a Markdown file at docs/hello.md:  docs/hello.md # Hello This is my **first Docusaurus document**!   A new document is now available at http://localhost:3000/docs/hello.

searchDocs.title: Configure the SidebarтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Docusaurus automatically creates a sidebar from the docs folder.  Add metadata to customize the sidebar label and position:  docs/hello.md --- sidebar_label: 'Hi!' sidebar_position: 3 --- # Hello This is my **first Docusaurus document**!   It is also possible to create your sidebar explicitly in sidebars.js:  sidebars.js export default { tutorialSidebar: [ 'intro', 'hello', { type: 'category', label: 'Tutorial', items: ['tutorial-basics/create-a-document'], }, ], };

searchDocs.title: Create a Page
searchDocs.keywords: QA ZERO  WordPress  analytics  heatmap  documentation

searchDocs.title: Create your first React PageтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Create a file at src/pages/my-react-page.js:  src/pages/my-react-page.js import React from 'react'; import Layout from '@theme/Layout'; export default function MyReactPage() { return ( <Layout> <h1>My React page</h1> <p>This is a React page</p> </Layout> ); }   A new page is now available at http://localhost:3000/my-react-page.

searchDocs.title: Create your first Markdown PageтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Create a file at src/pages/my-markdown-page.md:  src/pages/my-markdown-page.md # My Markdown page This is a Markdown page   A new page is now available at http://localhost:3000/my-markdown-page.

searchDocs.title: Deploy your site
searchDocs.keywords: QA ZERO  WordPress  analytics  heatmap  documentation

searchDocs.title: Build your siteтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Build your site for production:  npm run build   The static files are generated in the build folder.

searchDocs.title: Deploy your siteтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Test your production build locally:  npm run serve   The build folder is now served at http://localhost:3000/.  You can now deploy the build folder almost anywhere easily, for free or very small cost (read the Deployment Guide).

searchDocs.title: Manage Docs Versions
searchDocs.keywords: QA ZERO  WordPress  analytics  heatmap  documentation

searchDocs.title: Create a docs versionтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Release a version 1.0 of your project:  npm run docusaurus docs:version 1.0   The docs folder is copied into versioned_docs/version-1.0 and versions.json is created.  Your docs now have 2 versions:  1.0 at http://localhost:3000/docs/ for the version 1.0 docscurrent at http://localhost:3000/docs/next/ for the upcoming, unreleased docs

searchDocs.title: Add a Version DropdownтАЛ
searchDocs.content: To navigate seamlessly across versions, add a version dropdown.  Modify the docusaurus.config.js file:  docusaurus.config.js export default { themeConfig: { navbar: { items: [ { type: 'docsVersionDropdown', }, ], }, }, };   The docs version dropdown appears in your navbar:

searchDocs.title: Update an existing versionтАЛ
searchDocs.content: It is possible to edit versioned docs in their respective folder:  versioned_docs/version-1.0/hello.md updates http://localhost:3000/docs/hellodocs/hello.md updates http://localhost:3000/docs/next/hello

searchDocs.title: Translate your site
searchDocs.keywords: QA ZERO  WordPress  analytics  heatmap  documentation

searchDocs.title: Configure i18nтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Modify docusaurus.config.js to add support for the fr locale:  docusaurus.config.js export default { i18n: { defaultLocale: 'en', locales: ['en', 'fr'], }, };

searchDocs.title: Translate a docтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Copy the docs/intro.md file to the i18n/fr folder:  mkdir -p i18n/fr/docusaurus-plugin-content-docs/current/ cp docs/intro.md i18n/fr/docusaurus-plugin-content-docs/current/intro.md   Translate i18n/fr/docusaurus-plugin-content-docs/current/intro.md in French.

searchDocs.title: Start your localized siteтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Start your site on the French locale:  npm run start -- --locale fr   Your localized site is accessible at http://localhost:3000/fr/ and the Getting Started page is translated.  ц│ицДП In development, you can only use one locale at a time.

searchDocs.title: Add a Locale DropdownтАЛ
searchDocs.content: To navigate seamlessly across languages, add a locale dropdown.  Modify the docusaurus.config.js file:  docusaurus.config.js export default { themeConfig: { navbar: { items: [ { type: 'localeDropdown', }, ], }, }, };   The locale dropdown now appears in your navbar:

searchDocs.title: Build your localized siteтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Build your site for a specific locale:  npm run build -- --locale fr   Or build your site to include all the locales at once:  npm run build

searchDocs.title: Markdown Features
searchDocs.keywords: QA ZERO  WordPress  analytics  heatmap  documentation

searchDocs.title: Front MatterтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Markdown documents have metadata at the top called Front Matter:  my-doc.md --- id: my-doc-id title: My document title description: My document description slug: /my-custom-url --- ## Markdown heading Markdown text with [links](./hello.md)

searchDocs.title: LinksтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Regular Markdown links are supported, using url paths or relative file paths.  Let's see how to [Create a page](/create-a-page).   Let's see how to [Create a page](./create-a-page.md).   Result: Let's see how to Create a page.

searchDocs.title: ImagesтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Regular Markdown images are supported.  You can use absolute paths to reference images in the static directory (static/img/docusaurus.png):  ![Docusaurus logo](/img/docusaurus.png)     You can reference images relative to the current file as well. This is particularly useful to colocate images close to the Markdown files using them:  ![Docusaurus logo](./img/docusaurus.png)

searchDocs.title: Code BlocksтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Markdown code blocks are supported with Syntax highlighting.  ```jsx title="src/components/HelloDocusaurus.js" function HelloDocusaurus() { return <h1>Hello, Docusaurus!</h1>; } ```   src/components/HelloDocusaurus.js function HelloDocusaurus() { return <h1>Hello, Docusaurus!</h1>; }

searchDocs.title: AdmonitionsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Docusaurus has a special syntax to create admonitions and callouts:  :::tip[My tip] Use this awesome feature option ::: :::danger[Take care] This action is dangerous :::   My tip Use this awesome feature option  Take care This action is dangerous

searchDocs.title: MDX and React ComponentsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: MDX can make your documentation more interactive and allows using any React components inside Markdown:  export const Highlight = ({children, color}) => ( <span style={{ backgroundColor: color, borderRadius: '20px', color: '#fff', padding: '10px', cursor: 'pointer', }} onClick={() => { alert(`You clicked the color ${color} with label ${children}`) }}> {children} </span> ); This is <Highlight color="#25c2a0">Docusaurus green</Highlight> ! This is <Highlight color="#1877F2">Facebook blue</Highlight> !     This is Docusaurus green !  This is Facebook blue !

searchDocs.title: Analytics
searchDocs.keywords: QA ZERO  WordPress  analytics  heatmap  documentation

searchDocs.title: Dashboard OverviewтАЛ
searchDocs.content: The analytics dashboard gives you an overview of your website's performance, including:  Monthly session counts with predictionsGoal conversion trackingUser, session, and page view trendsInteractive charts and graphsReal-time data visualization

searchDocs.title: User AnalyticsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Analyze your website visitors with detailed metrics:  Total users, sessions, and page viewsNew vs. returning visitorsDevice type breakdown (PC, tablet, mobile)Bounce rate and exit pagesAverage session durationPage views per session

searchDocs.title: Session RecordingтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Replay actual user sessions to understand behavior:  Full session playback of user interactionsFilter recordings by conversion goalsView mouse movements and clicksAnalyze user journey and navigation pathsIdentify usability issues and drop-off points

searchDocs.title: Data FilteringтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Filter your analytics data by:  Custom date ranges with calendar pickerDevice type and operating systemTraffic source and referrersUTM parameters (source, medium, campaign)Page URL and content typeUser type (new or returning)

searchDocs.title: Data ExportтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Export your analytics data for further analysis:  TSV/CSV download of raw dataCustomizable date ranges for exportsComplete user journey informationDevice and browser detailsPage interaction metricsConversion data

searchDocs.title: Goal TrackingтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Monitor important conversion actions:  Track multiple conversion goalsView goal completion ratesAnalyze conversion trends over timeCompare goal performance by device typeIdentify high-converting traffic sources

searchDocs.title: Heatmaps
searchDocs.keywords: QA ZERO  WordPress  analytics  heatmap  documentation

searchDocs.title: Types of HeatmapsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Click Heatmaps: Shows where users click on your pages with heat intensity visualizationClick Count Maps: Displays numerical click counts on interactive elementsScroll Heatmaps: Visualizes how far users scroll down your pagesAttention Maps: Shows which areas of your page receive the most user attentionReading Level Maps: Visualizes content consumption and reading patterns

searchDocs.title: Device-Specific ViewsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: View heatmaps for different device types:  Desktop: Full-width view for desktop usersTablet: 768px width view for tablet usersMobile: 375px width view for mobile users

searchDocs.title: Advanced FilteringтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Filter your heatmap data by:  Date Range: Custom date selection with calendar pickerTraffic Source: Filter by referral domainsUTM Parameters: Filter by campaign, medium, and sourceGoal Completion: Filter to show only sessions that completed specific goals

searchDocs.title: Generating HeatmapsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: To generate a heatmap:  Navigate to the Heatmaps section in the QA ZERO dashboardSelect the page you want to analyzeChoose the device type (desktop, tablet, mobile)Select the heatmap type you want to viewSet the date range for data collectionApply any additional filters (source, campaign, goals)

searchDocs.title: Heatmap Analysis ToolsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: QA ZERO provides powerful tools for heatmap analysis:  Element Selectors: Identify specific HTML elements and their interaction metricsOverlay Controls: Toggle between different visualization typesVersion Comparison: Compare heatmaps across different page versionsData Export: Export heatmap data for further analysis

searchDocs.title: Analyzing HeatmapsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: When analyzing heatmaps, look for:  Areas with high engagement and attentionClickable elements that aren't being clickedContent that users aren't seeing (below the scroll fold)Differences in behavior across device typesCorrelation between attention patterns and goal completionReading patterns and content consumption metrics

searchDocs.title: Goals
searchDocs.keywords: QA ZERO  WordPress  analytics  heatmap  documentation

searchDocs.title: Setting Up GoalsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: To set up a goal:  Navigate to the Goals section in the QA ZERO dashboardClick "Add New Goal"Configure the goal parameters: Goal name and descriptionGoal value (for conversion value calculation)Completion target (monthly goal) Choose the goal type: Destination (specific page visit)Click (button/link interaction)Form submissionCustom event tracking Save the goal

searchDocs.title: Goal DashboardтАЛ
searchDocs.content: The Goals dashboard provides a comprehensive overview of your conversion performance:  Goal completions count for each goalGoal value calculations based on completed conversionsConversion rate percentage for each goalComparison of actual completions against monthly targetsConversion trend charts with session volume overlay

searchDocs.title: Goal AnalysisтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Analyze your goal performance with detailed breakdowns:

searchDocs.title: Source/Medium AnalysisтАЛ
searchDocs.content: View which traffic sources drive the most conversionsCompare conversion rates across different referrersAnalyze user engagement metrics by sourceIdentify high-value traffic channels

searchDocs.title: Landing Page AnalysisтАЛ
searchDocs.content: See which landing pages contribute to conversionsCompare new vs. returning user conversion ratesAnalyze bounce rates for converting pagesIdentify high-performing entry points

searchDocs.title: Visual Analysis ToolsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: QA ZERO provides visual tools to understand user behavior leading to conversions:

searchDocs.title: Heatmaps for Goal PagesтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Click heatmaps showing interaction patternsDevice-specific heatmaps (desktop, tablet, mobile)Filter heatmaps by conversion goalIdentify click patterns that lead to conversions

searchDocs.title: Session RecordingтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Replay actual user sessions that resulted in conversionsFilter recordings by specific goalsView the complete user journey from entry to conversionAnalyze user behavior patterns for successful conversions

searchDocs.title: Data Filtering and ExportтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Customize your goal analysis with powerful filtering options:  Filter by custom date rangesSegment by device type (desktop, tablet, mobile)Filter by traffic source and mediumExport conversion data for further analysisGenerate reports with conversion metrics

searchDocs.title: Session Recording
searchDocs.keywords: QA ZERO  WordPress  analytics  heatmap  documentation

searchDocs.title: How Session Recording WorksтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Session recording captures:  Mouse movements and cursor positionClicks and interactive elementsScrolling behavior and scroll depthPage navigation and multi-page journeysForm interactions and input fieldsTime spent on different sections of the pageBrowser resize events  All recordings are stored securely on your own server and can be accessed from your WordPress admin panel.

searchDocs.title: Viewing RecordingsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: To view session recordings:  Navigate to the Session Recording section in the QA ZERO dashboardBrowse the list of available recordings with filtering optionsClick on a recording to play itUse the comprehensive playback controls: Play/PauseRewind/Fast-forwardPlayback speed adjustment (0.5x to 2x)Timeline scrubbingSession navigation between pages

searchDocs.title: User Information DisplayтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Each recording provides detailed information about the visitor:  User Details: Anonymized ID, new/returning status, countryAccess Information: Landing time, referrer source, page view countDevice Information: Device type (desktop, tablet, mobile), operating system, browser

searchDocs.title: Session NavigationтАЛ
searchDocs.content: For multi-page sessions, QA ZERO provides:  Visual playlist of all pages visited by the userThumbnails and titles for each page in the journeyAutomatic playback of the next page in sequenceDirect navigation to any point in the user journey

searchDocs.title: Analysis ToolsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: QA ZERO provides powerful tools for analyzing session recordings:  Filtering Options: Filter recordings by: Date rangeDevice typeTraffic sourceGoal completionPage URLSession duration Integration with Other Features: View heatmaps for the same pageAnalyze conversion pathsIdentify usability issuesCorrelate with analytics data

searchDocs.title: Privacy ConsiderationsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Session recording is designed with privacy in mind:  Sensitive data like passwords and credit card numbers are automatically maskedForm input fields can be selectively excluded from recordingYou can configure which pages to record and which to excludeUser consent options are available to comply with privacy regulationsAll data is stored on your own server, not transmitted to third parties

searchDocs.title: License Activation
searchDocs.keywords: QA ZERO  WordPress  analytics  heatmap  documentation

searchDocs.title: Obtaining a License KeyтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Before you can activate QA ZERO premium, you need to purchase a license:  Visit the QA ZERO websiteSelect your preferred license tierComplete the checkout processYou will receive your license key via emailThe license key will also be available in your account dashboard

searchDocs.title: Standard ActivationтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Log in to your WordPress admin dashboardNavigate to QA ZERO > LicenseEnter your license key in the provided fieldEnter your email address in the provided fieldClick Activate the licenseIf successful, you'll see a confirmation message and a celebration animation

searchDocs.title: Troubleshooting ActivationтАЛ
searchDocs.content: If you encounter issues during activation:  Verify that you've entered the license key correctly (no extra spaces)Check your internet connectionEnsure your server can make outbound connectionsTemporarily disable security plugins or firewalls that might block the activation request  For more detailed troubleshooting, see the License Errors section.

searchDocs.title: Verifying ActivationтАЛ
searchDocs.content: After activation, you can verify that your license is working correctly:  Navigate to QA ZERO > DashboardCheck that premium features are now availableTry accessing a premium feature like session recording

searchDocs.title: License InformationтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Once activated, you can view your license information:  Navigate to QA ZERO > LicenseView your license details: Last authentication timeActivation status

searchDocs.title: Deactivating Your LicenseтАЛ
searchDocs.content: If you need to move your license to another site:  Navigate to QA ZERO > LicenseClick Deactivate this licenseConfirm the deactivationYour license can now be activated on another site

searchDocs.title: "Invalid License Key" ErrorтАЛ
searchDocs.content: This error occurs when:  The license key is entered incorrectlyThe license has expiredThe license has been revoked  Solution: Verify your license key and status in your QA ZERO account.

searchDocs.title: "License Activation Limit Reached" ErrorтАЛ
searchDocs.content: This error occurs when:  You've activated the license on the maximum number of sites allowed by your license tierYou're trying to activate a license on too many sites  Solution: Deactivate the license on an unused site.

searchDocs.title: "Connection Error" During ActivationтАЛ
searchDocs.content: This error occurs when:  Your server cannot connect to the QA ZERO license serverA firewall is blocking the connectionYour hosting has restrictions on outbound connections  Solution: Contact your hosting provider to ensure outbound connections are allowed.

searchDocs.title: Getting HelpтАЛ
searchDocs.content: If you continue to experience activation issues:  Check the QA ZERO Knowledge Base for license-related articlesContact QA ZERO support with: Your license key (last 4 characters only for security)WordPress versionQA ZERO plugin versionError messages you're receivingSteps you've already taken to troubleshoot

searchDocs.title: Free vs Premium Features
searchDocs.keywords: QA ZERO  WordPress  analytics  heatmap  documentation

searchDocs.title: Feature ComparisonтАЛ
searchDocs.content: The following table provides a comprehensive comparison of features available in the free and premium versions of QA ZERO:  Feature	Free Version	Premium VersionMeasured Pages	1 page only	Unlimited pages Analytics	Basic page views	Comprehensive analytics Heatmaps	Limited (click only)	All types (click, scroll, move, attention) Session Recording	тЭМ	тЬЕ Goal Tracking	Basic (1 goal)	Advanced (unlimited goals) Data Retention	30 days	Up to 1 year Export Options	Limited formats	All formats (CSV, TSV) Real-time Analytics	тЭМ	тЬЕ User Segmentation	тЭМ	тЬЕ Custom Reports	тЭМ	тЬЕ AI-powered Insights	тЭМ	тЬЕ Support	Community forum	Priority email support

searchDocs.title: Free Version DetailsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: The free version of QA ZERO includes:

searchDocs.title: Measurement LimitationsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Limited to tracking 1 page only (based on the FREE_MEASURE_PAGE constant)Basic functionality for that single page

searchDocs.title: Analytics FeaturesтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Basic page view trackingSimple visitor countBasic traffic sources

searchDocs.title: Heatmap FeaturesтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Click heatmaps onlyLimited to 1 active pageData collection for 30 days

searchDocs.title: LimitationsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: No session recordingNo AI-powered insightsLimited data export options (basic formats only)No custom reportsCommunity support only

searchDocs.title: Premium Version DetailsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: The premium version of QA ZERO includes everything in the free version, plus:

searchDocs.title: Unlimited MeasurementтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Track unlimited pages across your siteComprehensive data collection for all pagesFull site analytics and insights

searchDocs.title: Enhanced AnalyticsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Detailed visitor demographicsAdvanced traffic source analysisCustom date rangesUser flow visualizationConversion funnel analysis

searchDocs.title: Complete Heatmap SuiteтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Click heatmapsScroll heatmapsMouse movement heatmapsAttention heatmapsClick count mapsUnlimited active heatmapsData retention up to 1 year

searchDocs.title: Session RecordingтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Full visitor session recordingsSession filtering and searchEvent timelineTechnical informationMulti-page journey tracking

searchDocs.title: AI-powered InsightsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Automated data analysisTrend detectionAnomaly identificationActionable recommendationsPerformance predictions

searchDocs.title: Advanced FeaturesтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Real-time analyticsUser segmentationCustom reportsData export in multiple formats (CSV, TSV)API access for data integration

searchDocs.title: Premium TiersтАЛ
searchDocs.content: QA ZERO Premium is available in several tiers:

searchDocs.title: Personal TierтАЛ
searchDocs.content: 1 websiteAll premium featuresEmail support1 year data retention

searchDocs.title: Business TierтАЛ
searchDocs.content: 3 websitesAll premium featuresPriority support1 year data retentionAdvanced reporting

searchDocs.title: Business Plus TierтАЛ
searchDocs.content: 5 websitesAll premium featuresPriority support1 year data retentionAdvanced reportingEnhanced data analysis

searchDocs.title: Agency TierтАЛ
searchDocs.content: 10 websitesAll premium featuresDedicated support1 year data retentionClient management dashboard

searchDocs.title: Enterprise TierтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Custom number of websitesAll premium featuresDedicated supportExtended data retentionCustom implementation

searchDocs.title: Choosing the Right VersionтАЛ
searchDocs.content: The free version is ideal for:  Small websites with only one important page to trackBlog owners who want simple insights for a single pageUsers who want to try QA ZERO before upgrading  The premium version is recommended for:  Websites needing to track multiple pagesBusiness websites focused on conversion optimizationE-commerce sites needing detailed user behavior analysisMarketing professionals requiring comprehensive analyticsTeams wanting AI-powered insights and recommendationsAgencies managing multiple client websites

searchDocs.title: Upgrading to PremiumтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Ready to upgrade to QA ZERO Premium? Follow these steps:  Navigate to QA ZERO > License in your WordPress adminClick Upgrade to PremiumSelect your preferred premium tierComplete the checkout processYour license key will be automatically applied  For more information about activating your premium license, see the License Activation guide.

searchDocs.content: уГТуГ╝уГИуГЮуГГуГЧуГРуГ╝уВ╕уГзуГ│уБлуБдуБДуБж уБУуБоуГЪуГ╝уВ╕уБпуАБуГЙуВнуГеуГбуГ│уГИцзЛцИРуБошжЛчЫ┤уБЧуБлф╝┤уБДчз╗хЛХуБЧуБ╛уБЧуБЯуАВ цЬАцЦ░уБохЖЕхо╣уБпуБУуБбуВЙуВТуБФчв║шкНуБПуБауБХуБДя╝Ъ ЁЯСЙуГТуГ╝уГИуГЮуГГуГЧуГРуГ╝уВ╕уГзуГ│уБлуБдуБДуБж

searchDocs.title: Subscription Management
searchDocs.keywords: QA ZERO  WordPress  analytics  heatmap  documentation

searchDocs.title: Understanding Your SubscriptionтАЛ
searchDocs.content: QA ZERO premium licenses are subscription-based, with options for:  Annual billing (most popular)Monthly billingMulti-year billing (2-3 years with discount)  Your subscription includes:  Access to premium featuresPlugin updatesTechnical supportLicense management

searchDocs.title: Viewing Subscription DetailsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: To view your current subscription details:  Log in to your QA ZERO account at https://qazero.com/account/Navigate to the Subscriptions sectionView your subscription information: Plan type (Personal, Business, Agency)Billing cycle (monthly, annual, multi-year)Next billing datePayment methodSubscription history

searchDocs.title: Automatic RenewalтАЛ
searchDocs.content: By default, QA ZERO subscriptions are set to renew automatically:  You will receive email notifications 7 days and 3 days before the renewal dateThe renewal will use the payment method on fileYour license will continue without interruptionA receipt will be emailed to you after the renewal is processed

searchDocs.title: Manual RenewalтАЛ
searchDocs.content: If you've disabled automatic renewal:  Log in to your QA ZERO accountNavigate to the Subscriptions sectionClick Renew next to the expiring subscriptionSelect your renewal termComplete the checkout process

searchDocs.title: Renewal DiscountsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: QA ZERO offers loyalty discounts for renewals:  15% discount for 1-year renewals25% discount for 2-year renewals35% discount for 3-year renewals

searchDocs.title: Upgrading Your PlanтАЛ
searchDocs.content: To upgrade to a higher tier (e.g., from Personal to Business):  Log in to your QA ZERO accountNavigate to the Subscriptions sectionClick Upgrade next to your current subscriptionSelect your new plan tierReview the prorated amount (credit for unused portion of current subscription)Complete the checkout processYour license will be immediately upgraded

searchDocs.title: Downgrading Your PlanтАЛ
searchDocs.content: To downgrade to a lower tier:  Log in to your QA ZERO accountNavigate to the Subscriptions sectionClick Change PlanSelect a lower tierChoose when to apply the change: Immediately (prorated refund issued)At next renewal date (current plan remains active until then) Confirm your selection  Note that downgrading may result in loss of access to certain features and reduced site limits.

searchDocs.title: Changing Billing CycleтАЛ
searchDocs.content: To change from monthly to annual billing (or vice versa):  Log in to your QA ZERO accountNavigate to the Subscriptions sectionClick Change Billing CycleSelect your preferred cycleReview the price adjustmentConfirm the change

searchDocs.title: Adding a Payment MethodтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Log in to your QA ZERO accountNavigate to the Payment Methods sectionClick Add Payment MethodEnter your payment detailsSet as default if desiredSave the payment method

searchDocs.title: Updating a Payment MethodтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Log in to your QA ZERO accountNavigate to the Payment Methods sectionFind the payment method you want to updateClick EditUpdate the informationSave changes

searchDocs.title: Removing a Payment MethodтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Log in to your QA ZERO accountNavigate to the Payment Methods sectionFind the payment method you want to removeClick DeleteConfirm deletion  Note: You cannot delete a payment method that is currently being used for an active subscription.

searchDocs.title: Cancellation ProcessтАЛ
searchDocs.content: To cancel your QA ZERO subscription:  Log in to your QA ZERO accountNavigate to the Subscriptions sectionClick Cancel SubscriptionSelect a reason for cancellation (optional but helpful)Choose when to cancel: Immediately (prorated refund may be issued)At the end of the current billing period Confirm cancellation

searchDocs.title: What Happens After CancellationтАЛ
searchDocs.content: After cancelling your subscription:  You will receive a confirmation emailIf cancelled immediately: Premium features will be disabledYour site will revert to the free versionCollected data will be retained for 30 days If cancelled at the end of the billing period: Premium features will remain active until that dateYou will receive a reminder email before features are disabled

searchDocs.title: Reactivating a Cancelled SubscriptionтАЛ
searchDocs.content: To reactivate a cancelled subscription:  Log in to your QA ZERO accountNavigate to the Subscriptions sectionFind your cancelled subscriptionClick ReactivateConfirm reactivation  If reactivated within 30 days, your previous settings and data will be restored.

searchDocs.title: Can I use my license on multiple sites?тАЛ
searchDocs.content: Personal tier: 1 site onlyBusiness tier: Up to 3 sitesAgency tier: Up to 10 sites

searchDocs.title: What happens if my payment fails?тАЛ
searchDocs.content: We will attempt to process the payment again after 3 daysYou will receive email notifications about the failed paymentAfter three failed attempts, your subscription will be pausedYou will have a 15-day grace period to update your payment information

searchDocs.title: Can I get a refund?тАЛ
searchDocs.content: QA ZERO offers a 14-day money-back guarantee. Contact support within 14 days of purchase for a full refund.

searchDocs.title: How do I get an invoice for my subscription?тАЛ
searchDocs.content: Invoices are automatically emailed after each payment. You can also download them from the Billing History section of your account.

searchDocs.title: Getting HelpтАЛ
searchDocs.content: If you have questions about your subscription:  Check the Billing FAQContact the billing team at billing@qazero.comUse the live chat on the QA ZERO website during business hours

searchDocs.title: Plugin Compatibility
searchDocs.keywords: QA ZERO  WordPress  analytics  heatmap  documentation

searchDocs.title: Compatible Caching PluginsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: QA ZERO works well with most popular caching plugins, including:  WP RocketW3 Total CacheWP Super CacheLiteSpeed CacheAutoptimize

searchDocs.title: Potential IssuesтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Caching plugins may cache the QA ZERO tracking code, which can lead to inaccurate data collection. To prevent this:  Exclude QA ZERO Scripts: Configure your caching plugin to exclude QA ZERO JavaScript files from being cached or minified.Dynamic Content: Ensure your caching plugin is configured to handle dynamic content correctly.

searchDocs.title: Configuration StepsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: WP RocketтАЛ  Go to WP Rocket > Advanced RulesAdd the following to the "Never Cache URLs" section: /wp-content/plugins/qa-zero/ Add the following to the "Excluded JavaScript Files" section: /wp-content/plugins/qa-zero/js/ Save changes  W3 Total CacheтАЛ  Go to Performance > MinifyAdd the following to the "Never minify the following JS files" section: /wp-content/plugins/qa-zero/js/ Save changes

searchDocs.title: Compatible Security PluginsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: QA ZERO is compatible with most security plugins, including:  WordfenceSucuriiThemes SecurityAll In One WP Security

searchDocs.title: Potential IssuesтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Some security plugins may block QA ZERO's data collection as suspicious activity. To prevent this:  Whitelist QA ZERO: Add QA ZERO's scripts and API endpoints to your security plugin's whitelist.Adjust Firewall Rules: Modify firewall rules to allow QA ZERO's necessary operations.

searchDocs.title: Configuration StepsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: WordfenceтАЛ  Go to Wordfence > FirewallClick on "Allowlisted URLs"Add the following patterns: /wp-content/plugins/qa-zero/ /wp-admin/admin-ajax.php Save changes

searchDocs.title: Compatible SEO PluginsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: QA ZERO works well with all major SEO plugins, including:  Yoast SEORank MathAll in One SEOSEOPress  No special configuration is typically needed for SEO plugins.

searchDocs.title: Compatible Page BuildersтАЛ
searchDocs.content: QA ZERO is compatible with popular page builders, including:  ElementorBeaver BuilderDivi BuilderWPBakery Page BuilderGutenberg (WordPress Block Editor)

searchDocs.title: Potential IssuesтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Some page builders may use custom JavaScript that conflicts with QA ZERO's tracking. If you notice issues:  Test in Safe Mode: Many page builders offer a safe mode or debug mode. Test QA ZERO with this enabled.Update to Latest Versions: Ensure both QA ZERO and your page builder are updated to the latest versions.

searchDocs.title: Compatible Form PluginsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: QA ZERO works with most form plugins, including:  Contact Form 7WPFormsGravity FormsNinja FormsFormidable Forms

searchDocs.title: Special Integration FeaturesтАЛ
searchDocs.content: QA ZERO can track form submissions as conversion goals:  Navigate to QA ZERO > GoalsClick Add New GoalSelect "Form Submission" as the goal typeSelect your form plugin and specific formSave the goal

searchDocs.title: Compatible PluginsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: QA ZERO is compatible with:  WooCommerceEasy Digital DownloadsMemberPressLearnDashWP-Members

searchDocs.title: Special Integration FeaturesтАЛ
searchDocs.content: QA ZERO offers enhanced tracking for e-commerce activities:  Navigate to QA ZERO > Settings > IntegrationsEnable the integration for your e-commerce or membership pluginConfigure tracking options for purchases, registrations, or course completions

searchDocs.title: Troubleshooting Plugin ConflictsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: If you suspect a plugin conflict with QA ZERO:  Temporarily Disable Plugins: Disable other plugins one by one to identify which one is causing the conflict.Check Browser Console: Look for JavaScript errors in your browser's developer console.Update All Plugins: Ensure all plugins are updated to their latest versions.Contact Support: If you can't resolve the issue, contact QA ZERO support with details about the conflict.  For specific plugin conflict issues not covered here, please refer to the Troubleshooting section.

searchDocs.title: Site Performance
searchDocs.keywords: QA ZERO  WordPress  analytics  heatmap  documentation

searchDocs.title: Performance ImpactтАЛ
searchDocs.content: QA ZERO's impact on site performance is typically minimal:  The tracking script is lightweight (< 50KB)Scripts load asynchronously to avoid blocking page renderingData is sent to the server in the backgroundProcessing occurs on the server, not in the user's browser

searchDocs.title: Measured ImpactтАЛ
searchDocs.content: In our testing across various website types:  Metric	Average ImpactPage Load Time	+0.1 to 0.3 seconds First Contentful Paint	Negligible Time to Interactive	Negligible Lighthouse Performance Score	-1 to -3 points

searchDocs.title: Basic OptimizationтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Navigate to QA ZERO > Settings > PerformanceEnable Optimize Script LoadingSet Tracking Precision to "Balanced" (default)Save changes

searchDocs.title: Advanced OptimizationтАЛ
searchDocs.content: For sites where performance is critical:  Navigate to QA ZERO > Settings > PerformanceEnable Minimal Tracking ModeConfigure which tracking features to enable/disable: Click TrackingScroll TrackingMouse Movement TrackingSession Recording Save changes  Note that disabling certain tracking features will limit the data available in your reports.

searchDocs.title: Caching CompatibilityтАЛ
searchDocs.content: QA ZERO is designed to work with caching plugins:  The tracking script includes cache-busting parametersUser-specific data is handled via cookies and localStorageThe admin interface is automatically excluded from caching  For optimal performance with caching:  Navigate to QA ZERO > Settings > AdvancedEnable Cache Compatibility ModeSave changes  See the Plugin Compatibility section for specific caching plugin configurations.

searchDocs.title: CDN CompatibilityтАЛ
searchDocs.content: QA ZERO works with Content Delivery Networks (CDNs):  Navigate to QA ZERO > Settings > PerformanceEnable CDN CompatibilityIf your CDN requires specific configuration, enter the details in the provided fieldsSave changes

searchDocs.title: Database OptimizationтАЛ
searchDocs.content: QA ZERO stores collected data in your WordPress database. To optimize database performance:  Navigate to QA ZERO > Settings > DatabaseConfigure Data Retention Period (shorter periods mean less database storage)Set Database Cleanup Schedule (daily, weekly, or monthly)Enable Database Optimization to automatically optimize tablesSave changes

searchDocs.title: Reducing Server LoadтАЛ
searchDocs.content: For high-traffic websites:  Navigate to QA ZERO > Settings > PerformanceEnable Sampling Mode to track only a percentage of visitorsConfigure the sampling rate (e.g., 50% means only half of visitors are tracked)Enable Rate Limiting to prevent traffic spikes from overwhelming your serverSave changes

searchDocs.title: Mobile OptimizationтАЛ
searchDocs.content: To optimize performance for mobile users:  Navigate to QA ZERO > Settings > PerformanceEnable Mobile OptimizationConfigure Mobile Tracking Level (Full, Balanced, or Minimal)Save changes

searchDocs.title: Performance MonitoringтАЛ
searchDocs.content: QA ZERO includes tools to monitor its own performance impact:  Navigate to QA ZERO > Settings > PerformanceView the Performance Impact sectionCheck the Script Load Time and Processing Time metricsIf these values are higher than the benchmarks, consider applying the optimization techniques described above

searchDocs.title: Best PracticesтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Start with default settings and optimize only if neededUse sampling for high-traffic sites (100,000+ monthly pageviews)Regularly check performance metrics in your hosting dashboardConsider upgrading hosting if your site is already resource-constrainedBalance data collection needs with performance requirements

searchDocs.title: Troubleshooting Performance IssuesтАЛ
searchDocs.content: If you notice performance issues after installing QA ZERO:  Temporarily disable QA ZERO to confirm it's the source of the problemEnable Debug Mode in QA ZERO settings to identify specific issuesCheck browser console for JavaScript errorsReview server logs for PHP errors or slow queriesContact QA ZERO support if issues persist after applying optimization techniques  For persistent performance issues, please refer to the Troubleshooting section.

searchDocs.title: CookieхРМцДПуГДуГ╝уГлуБиуБощАгцР║я╝ИуВ╣уВпуГкуГЧуГИшинч╜оя╝Й
searchDocs.content: CookieхРМцДПуГДуГ╝уГлуБиуБощАгцР║я╝ИуВ╣уВпуГкуГЧуГИшинч╜оя╝Й уБУуБоуГЪуГ╝уВ╕уБпуАБуГЙуВнуГеуГбуГ│уГИцзЛцИРуБошжЛчЫ┤уБЧуБлф╝┤уБДчз╗хЛХуБЧуБ╛уБЧуБЯуАВ цЬАцЦ░уБохЖЕхо╣уБпуБУуБбуВЙуВТуБФчв║шкНуБПуБауБХуБДя╝Ъ ЁЯСЙCookieхРМцДПуГДуГ╝уГлуБиуБощАгцР║я╝ИуВ╣уВпуГкуГЧуГИшинч╜оя╝Й
searchDocs.keywords: QA ZERO  WordPress  analytics  heatmap  documentation

searchDocs.title: Theme Layout Issues
searchDocs.keywords: QA ZERO  WordPress  analytics  heatmap  documentation

searchDocs.title: Heatmap Overlay MisalignmentтАЛ
searchDocs.content: The most common theme-related issue is misalignment between the heatmap overlay and the actual website content.    Possible causes:  Dynamic Content: Elements that load after the initial page load (sliders, lazy-loaded images)Responsive Layouts: Themes that significantly alter layout across different screen sizesCustom Positioning: Elements with absolute or fixed positioningiFrames: Content loaded within iFrames

searchDocs.title: Solution StepsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Navigate to QA ZERO > Settings > AdvancedEnable Delayed Tracking and set the delay to 2-3 secondsEnable Enhanced Element TrackingSave changes and test the heatmap again  For persistent issues:  Navigate to QA ZERO > Settings > AdvancedIn the Custom Code section, add the following JavaScript: window.qaZeroConfig = { adjustForDynamicContent: true, trackingDelay: 2500, resizeThrottle: 500 }; Save changes and test the heatmap again

searchDocs.title: WordPress Default ThemesтАЛ
searchDocs.content: QA ZERO works well with all default WordPress themes (Twenty Twenty, Twenty Twenty-One, etc.) without any special configuration.

searchDocs.title: Popular Theme FrameworksтАЛ
searchDocs.content: DiviтАЛ  For Divi themes:  Navigate to QA ZERO > Settings > AdvancedEnable Divi Compatibility ModeSave changes  AvadaтАЛ  For Avada themes:  Navigate to QA ZERO > Settings > AdvancedEnable Fusion Builder CompatibilitySave changes  AstraтАЛ  For Astra themes:  Ensure you're using Astra 3.0 or higherNo special configuration is typically needed  GeneratePressтАЛ  QA ZERO works well with GeneratePress without any special configuration.

searchDocs.title: Fixing Z-index IssuesтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Some themes use high z-index values that may cause elements to appear above the QA ZERO interface.  To fix this:  Navigate to QA ZERO > Settings > AdvancedEnable Force Interface LayerAdjust the Interface Z-Index value if needed (default is 9999)Save changes

searchDocs.title: Handling Custom Post TypesтАЛ
searchDocs.content: If your theme uses custom post types with unique layouts:  Navigate to QA ZERO > Settings > TrackingUnder Content Types, ensure your custom post types are selectedClick Advanced SettingsConfigure tracking settings specifically for each custom post typeSave changes

searchDocs.title: Mobile Theme CompatibilityтАЛ
searchDocs.content: If your site uses a separate mobile theme or significantly different mobile layout:  Navigate to QA ZERO > Settings > AdvancedEnable Enhanced Mobile TrackingConfigure the Mobile Breakpoint value to match your theme's mobile breakpointSave changes

searchDocs.title: AMP Pages CompatibilityтАЛ
searchDocs.content: For themes that support AMP (Accelerated Mobile Pages):  Navigate to QA ZERO > Settings > IntegrationsEnable AMP Compatibility ModeChoose whether to track AMP pages separately or combine with regular page dataSave changes

searchDocs.title: Custom Theme HooksтАЛ
searchDocs.content: If your theme uses custom action hooks for header and footer:  Navigate to QA ZERO > Settings > AdvancedIn the Custom Hooks section, enter your theme's header and footer hook namesSave changes

searchDocs.title: Testing After Theme UpdatesтАЛ
searchDocs.content: After updating your theme:  Visit your website and check that QA ZERO tracking is still workingView a heatmap to ensure proper alignmentTest on multiple devices to ensure responsive compatibility  If issues occur after a theme update:  Navigate to QA ZERO > Settings > AdvancedClick Reset Compatibility SettingsFollow the setup wizard to reconfigure theme compatibility

searchDocs.title: Getting Help with Theme IssuesтАЛ
searchDocs.content: If you continue to experience theme-related issues:  Check if your theme has specific documentation for analytics plugin compatibilityContact your theme developer to ask about compatibility with heatmap and analytics toolsContact QA ZERO support with details about your theme and the specific issues you're experiencing

searchDocs.title: шиИц╕муВ┐уВ░уБошинч╜о
searchDocs.content: шиИц╕муВ┐уВ░уБошинч╜о уБУуБоуГЪуГ╝уВ╕уБпуАБуГЙуВнуГеуГбуГ│уГИцзЛцИРуБошжЛчЫ┤уБЧуБлф╝┤уБДчз╗хЛХуБЧуБ╛уБЧуБЯуАВ цЬАцЦ░уБохЖЕхо╣уБпуБУуБбуВЙуВТуБФчв║шкНуБПуБауБХуБДя╝Ъ ЁЯСЙшиИц╕муВ┐уВ░уБошинч╜о

searchDocs.content: уБДуБДуБИуАВQA ZERO уБпуАБWordPress уВТуГЯуГЙуГлуВжуВзуВвуБиуБЧуБжхИйчФиуБЩуВЛчЛмчлЛхЮЛуБохИЖцЮРуВ╡уГ╝уГРуГ╝уВ╜уГкуГеуГ╝уВ╖уГзуГ│уБзуБЩуАВ  хИйф╛┐цАзуБоуБЯуВБуБл WordPress уГЧуГйуВ░уВдуГ│х╜вх╝ПуБзщЕНх╕ГуБХуВМуБжуБДуБ╛уБЩуБМуАБхоЯщЪЫуБлуБпчЛмчлЛуБЧуБЯхИЖцЮРуВиуГ│уВ╕уГ│уБиуБЧуБжхЛХф╜ЬуБЧуБ╛уБЩуАВWordPress уБпф╕╗уБлуВдуГ│уВ╣уГИуГ╝уГлуБКуВИуБ│чобчРЖуВдуГ│уВ┐уГ╝уГХуВзуГ╝уВ╣уБох╜╣хЙ▓уВТцЛЕуБгуБжуБДуБ╛уБЩуАВ

searchDocs.content: QA ZERO уБпуАБуВдуГ│уВ╣уГИуГ╝уГлуБохо╣цШУуБХуБичобчРЖчФ╗щЭвуБоцПРф╛ЫуБоуБЯуВБуБл WordPress уВТф╜┐чФиуБЧуБжуБДуБ╛уБЩуАВ  уБУуВМуБлуВИуВКуАБх╝╖хКЫуБкхИЖцЮРцйЯшГ╜уВТцПРф╛ЫуБЧуБкуБМуВЙуВВуАБх░ОхЕеуВДчобчРЖуВТцпФш╝ГчЪДуВ╖уГ│уГЧуГлуБлшбМуБЖуБУуБиуБМуБзуБНуБ╛уБЩуАВ

searchDocs.content: уБпуБДуАВQA ZERO уБпуГЖуГ╝уГЮуБиуБпхЯ║цЬмчЪДуБлчЛмчлЛуБЧуБжхЛХф╜ЬуБЩуВЛуБЯуВБуАБуБ╗уБиуВУуБйуБо WordPress уГЖуГ╝уГЮуБиф║ТцПЫцАзуБМуБВуВКуБ╛уБЩуАВ  чЙ╣хоЪуБошбичд║х┤йуВМуВДхЛХф╜ЬхХПщбМуБМуБВуВЛха┤хРИуБпуАБTroubleshooting уВ╗уВпуВ╖уГзуГ│уВТуБФчв║шкНуБПуБауБХуБДуАВ

searchDocs.content: QA ZERO уБпуГСуГХуВйуГ╝уГЮуГ│уВ╣уБлщЕНцЕоуБЧуБжшиншиИуБХуВМуБжуБДуБ╛уБЩуАВ  уГИуГйуГГуВнуГ│уВ░уВ╣уВпуГкуГЧуГИуБпш╗╜щЗПуБЛуБдщЭЮхРМцЬЯуБзшкнуБ┐ш╛╝уБ╛уВМуАБхИЖцЮРхЗжчРЖуБпчЛмчлЛуБЧуБЯуВ╡уГ╝уГРуГ╝хБ┤уБзшбМуВПуВМуБ╛уБЩуАВщАЪх╕╕уАБуВ╡уВдуГИшбичд║щАЯх║жуБ╕уБох╜▒щЯ┐уБпцЬАх░ПщЩРуБзуБЩуАВ

searchDocs.content: уБпуБДуАБхдЪуБПуБоф╕АшИмчЪДуБкуВнуГгуГГуВ╖уГеуГЧуГйуВ░уВдуГ│уБиф╜╡чФихПпшГ╜уБзуБЩуАВ  уБЯуБауБЧуАБшиИц╕муВ┐уВ░уБохЛХф╜ЬуБлх╜▒щЯ┐уБМхЗ║уВЛха┤хРИуБМуБВуВКуБ╛уБЩуАВхХПщбМуБМчЩ║чФЯуБЩуВЛха┤хРИуБпуВнуГгуГГуВ╖уГешинхоЪуВТуБФчв║шкНуБПуБауБХуБДуАВ

searchDocs.content: ф╛Ля╝Ъ чЫоциЩчФ╗щЭвуБзуБп 400уВ╗уГГуВ╖уГзуГ│ уБишбичд║уБХуВМуБжуБДуВЛуБоуБлуАБ уГТуГ╝уГИуГЮуГГуГЧуБзуБп 1200уГЗуГ╝уВ┐ уБишбичд║уБХуВМуВЛха┤хРИуБМуБВуВКуБ╛уБЩуАВ

searchDocs.content: чЫоциЩчФ╗щЭвуБошбиуБлшбичд║уБХуВМуВЛуАМуВ╗уГГуВ╖уГзуГ│уАНуБпуАБ хоЯщЪЫуБлуБп уГТуГ╝уГИуГЮуГГуГЧуГЗуГ╝уВ┐уБМшиШщМ▓уБХуВМуБЯуГЪуГ╝уВ╕уГУуГеуГ╝я╝ИPVя╝ЙцХ░уБохРИшиИ уВТшбичд║уБЧуБжуБДуБ╛уБЩуАВ  уГйуГЩуГлф╕КуБпуАМуВ╗уГГуВ╖уГзуГ│уАНуБиуБкуБгуБжуБДуБ╛уБЩуБМуАБ хЖЕщГихЗжчРЖуБзуБп уГТуГ╝уГИуГЮуГГуГЧхп╛ш▒буБиуБкуВЛцЬЙхК╣PVцХ░ уВТщЫЖшиИуБЧуБжуБДуБ╛уБЩуАВ

searchDocs.title: Troubleshooting Data Loss
searchDocs.keywords: QA ZERO  WordPress  analytics  heatmap  documentation

searchDocs.title: Complete Data LossтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Complete data loss occurs when all historical data disappears from your QA ZERO dashboard.  Possible causes:  Database Reset: Accidental use of the "Reset Data" functionPlugin Reinstallation: Uninstalling and reinstalling the plugin without proper backupDatabase Corruption: Server issues affecting the WordPress database

searchDocs.title: Partial Data LossтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Partial data loss occurs when some data is missing for specific time periods or pages.  Possible causes:  Tracking Interruption: Temporary issues with the tracking scriptData Processing Errors: Background processing failuresData Retention Settings: Automatic deletion of older dataContent Filtering: Exclusion rules preventing data collection

searchDocs.title: Check Data Collection StatusтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Navigate to QA ZERO > DashboardCheck the dashboard for recent dataIf recent data is visible, data collection is currently working

searchDocs.title: Verify Recent DataтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Navigate to QA ZERO > UserSet the date range to "Today" or "Yesterday"Check if recent data is being recordedIf recent data exists but historical data is missing, the issue is likely with stored data rather than collection

searchDocs.title: Check WordPress DatabaseтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Use a database management tool like phpMyAdminCheck the WordPress database tables related to QA ZEROVerify that the tables exist and contain data

searchDocs.title: Review WordPress Error LogsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Check your WordPress error logsLook for errors related to QA ZERONote the timestamps of any relevant errors

searchDocs.title: Data Retention SettingsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: QA ZERO automatically removes old data based on your retention settings:  Navigate to QA ZERO > SettingsSelect the Site Management tabCheck the Data Retention Period settingIf set to a short period (e.g., 30 days), older data will be automatically deletedAdjust the setting to a longer period if neededNote that changing this setting will not restore already deleted data

searchDocs.title: Database IssuesтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Database corruption can cause data loss:  Use a database management tool like phpMyAdminCheck the WordPress database tables related to QA ZERORepair or optimize the database tables if needed  For severe database issues:  Check your server's error logs for database-related errorsContact your hosting provider for assistance with database repairRestore from a backup if available

searchDocs.title: Tracking Script IssuesтАЛ
searchDocs.content: If the tracking script was temporarily disabled or broken:  Navigate to QA ZERO > TagVerify that the tracking code is properly installedIf using a caching plugin, clear your cacheCheck your site again to see if tracking is working

searchDocs.title: Plugin ConflictsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Other plugins may interfere with QA ZERO's data collection or storage:  Temporarily deactivate other plugins one by oneAfter each deactivation, check if QA ZERO data collection worksIf you identify a conflicting plugin, check the Plugin Compatibility section for specific guidance

searchDocs.title: Server Resource LimitationsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Limited server resources can prevent data processing:  Check your server's PHP memory limit and execution timeEnsure your server meets the minimum requirements (PHP 5.6+, 64MB memory)If resources are limited, contact your hosting provider about upgrading

searchDocs.title: From BackupтАЛ
searchDocs.content: If you have a backup of your WordPress database:  Restore the database from backupVerify that QA ZERO tables are included in the backupReactivate the plugin if necessary

searchDocs.title: Using WordPress Database ToolsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: If you need to recover data from a corrupted database:  Use a database management tool like phpMyAdminCheck the WordPress database tables related to QA ZERORepair or optimize the database tables if neededIf necessary, consult with a database administrator

searchDocs.title: From Browser CacheтАЛ
searchDocs.content: In some cases, limited data may still exist in visitors' browsers:  Note that QA ZERO uses localStorage for some temporary dataThis data is limited and may not represent a complete recovery solutionFor reliable data recovery, always maintain regular database backups

searchDocs.title: Regular BackupsтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Set up regular database backups:  Use a WordPress backup plugin or your hosting provider's backup toolsEnsure that database backups include QA ZERO tablesConfigure backup frequency and retentionStore backups in a secure location (local or cloud)

searchDocs.title: Optimize Server ResourcesтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Reduce the risk of data processing failures:  Ensure your server meets the minimum requirements (PHP 5.6+, 64MB memory)Consider upgrading your hosting plan if you have a high-traffic siteRegularly check server logs for errors or warningsKeep WordPress and all plugins updated

searchDocs.title: Monitor Site PerformanceтАЛ
searchDocs.content: Monitor your site's performance:  Regularly check the QA ZERO dashboard for any anomaliesUse WordPress health check tools to monitor overall site healthSet up uptime monitoring for your websiteRegularly check for plugin conflicts

searchDocs.title: Getting SupportтАЛ
searchDocs.content: If you've tried the troubleshooting steps above and still have issues:  Gather information about your system: WordPress versionPHP versionServer environmentQA ZERO plugin version Contact QA ZERO support with: Your system informationDescription of the data lossTime period affectedSteps you've already taken to troubleshoot
